MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-03-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY & JEFFREY RIZZO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS MET THEIR INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT THEY DID NOT BREACH A DUTY OF CARE TO PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WHETHER, DESPITE THE UNDISPUTED FACTS THAT DEFENDANT RIZZO HAD THE GREEN LIGHT AND THE RIGHT OF WAY AT THE TIME OF THE COLLISION, HE BREACHED A DUTY OF CARE OWED TO PLAINTIFF. SEE LEO V. DUNHAM (1953) 41 CAL. 2D 712, 715 ("GENERAL RULE IS THAT EVERY PERSON HAS A RIGHT TO PRESUME THAT EVERY OTHER PERSON WILL PERFORM HIS DUTY AND OBEY THE LAW, AND IN THE ABSENCE OF REASONABLE GROUND TO THINK OTHERWISE IT IS NOT NEGLIGENCE TO ASSUME THAT HE IS NOT EXPOSED TO DANGER WHICH COMES TO HIM ONLY FROM VIOLATION OF LAW OR DUTY BY SUCH OTHER PERSON")(EMPHASIS ADDED). SEE DECLARATION OF ALVIN LOWI IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO MOTION, PARAS. 4-6); DECLARATION OF ANDREW BRYMAN IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO MOTION, PARA. 2, EX. 2 (DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT JEFFREY RIZZO), AT 13:13-24; 15:17-23; 17:12-19:7; 19:14-24; 40:22-24; 43:14-44:8; 45:3-46:4; 46:23-47:18; 49:3-6; 51:25-52:3; 55:16-56:14; 57:18-20; 58:4-22; 66:4-11; 77:8-78:5; 79:22-80:9; 100:10-21;102:18-103:6; 103:15-23; 104:14-18; 23-25. THE COURT RULES ON DEFENDANTS' EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS FOLLOWS: OBJECTIONS TO DECLARATION OF ALVIN LOWI: OVERRULED; OBJECTIONS TO DECLARATION OF ANDREW C. BRYMAN: SUSTAINED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Petition To Approve Compromise Of Claim_1204497
Off Calendar Jul-12-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for a Hearing on a Compromise of Claim.
Department 514, Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: A. Mok, CSR: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

Andrew C. Bryman, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff and Petitioner: Georgina Paige, mother and guardian ad litem for incompetent person: James Vincent Dawson appearing through court call.
Petitioner sworn and examined.

After testimony and consideration of the evidence presented, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved as follows:
Total Settlement: $167,500.00
Attorney's Fees: $41,875.00 payable to Law Firm of Bryman & Apelian
Costs: $127,758.82
A net balance of $39,741.18 to be paid to Georgina Marie Page, Trustee of James Vincent Dawson Special Needs Trust dated 2/11/2014.

Court signed Order Granting approval of Compromise of Claim for an incompetent person.
Status Hearing Scheduled on 10/6/2016 at 9:00 AM, in Department 514 for filing of receipt for the deposit of money into a Special Needs Trust.
No appearance is required if the receipt is filed and courtesy copy lodged with Dept. 514 prior to Hearing.

Mtn To File An Undertaking
Off Calendar Jan-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DEBRA FISCHL, ESQ., 818.225.5151, FOR PLANITIFF JAMES DAWSON BY HIS GUARDIAN AD LITEM GEORGINIA PAGE.
GARY NEVOLO, ESQ., 415.617.2249, FOR DEFENDNATS JEFFREY RIZZO AND OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY.

RULING: MATTER DISCUSSED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE HAS BEEN A SUFFICIENT SHOW OF PLAINTIFF'S INDIGENCY. DEFENDANTS OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY & JEFFREY RIZZO'S MOTION To File An Undertaking IS DENIED. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Mtn To Bifurcate
Off Calendar Jan-03-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DEBRA FISCHL OF THE LAW FIRM OF BRYMAN AND APELIAN, TEL. NO. (818) 225-5151 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. BART L. KESSEL & ATTY. GARY J. NOVELO OF THE TUCKER ELLIS LLP, TEL. NO. 617-2209 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY & JEFFREY RIZZO's Motion To Bifurcate Trial of the Issue of Liability from Damages at trial is Denied without prejudice to raising the issue before the trial judge. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-03-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DEBRA FISCHL OF THE LAW FIRM OF BRYMAN AND APELIAN, TEL. NO. (818) 225-5151 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. BART L. KESSEL & ATTY. GARY J. NOVELO OF THE TUCKER ELLIS LLP, TEL. NO. (617-2209 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY & JEFFREY RIZZO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS denied. Defendants met their initial burden of production that they did not breach a duty of care to Plaintiff. Plaintiff has raised a triable issue of material fact whether, despite the undisputed facts that Defendant Rizzo had the green light and the right of way at the time of the collision, he breached a duty of care owed to Plaintiff. See Leo v. Dunham (1953) 41 Cal. 2d 712, 715 ("general rule is that every person has a right to presume that every other person will perform his duty and obey the law, and in the absence of reasonable ground to think otherwise it is not negligence to assume that he is not exposed to danger which comes to him only from violation of law or duty by such other person")(emphasis added). See Declaration of Alvin Lowi in support of Plaintiff's opposition to motion, paras. 4-6); Declaration of Andrew Bryman in support of Plaintiff's opposition to motion, para. 2, Ex. 2 (Deposition of Defendant Jeffrey Rizzo), at 13:13-24; 15:17-23; 17:12-19:7; 19:14-24; 40:22-24; 43:14-44:8; 45:3-46:4; 46:23-47:18; 49:3-6; 51:25-52:3; 55:16-56:14; 57:18-20; 58:4-22; 66:4-11; 77:8-78:5; 79:22-80:9; 100:10-21;102:18-103:6; 103:15-23; 104:14-18; 23-25. The Court rules on Defendants' Evidentiary Objections to Plaintiff's Opposition to Motion for Summary Judgment as follows: objections to Declaration of Alvin Lowi: overruled; objections to Declaration of Andrew C. Bryman: sustained. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

Mtn To File An Undertaking
Off Calendar Jan-03-2014 Continued To Jan-10-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DEBRA FISCHL OF THE LAW FIRM OF BRYMAN AND APELIAN, TEL. NO. (818) 225-5151 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. BART L. KESSEL & ATTY. GARY J. NOVELO OF THE TUCKER ELLIS LLP, TEL. NO. (617-2209 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - DEFENDANTS OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY & JEFFREY RIZZO's Motion To File An Undertaking is continued to January 10, 2014. Plaintiff is requested to provide further information regarding Plaintiff's inability to post an undertaking on account of indigence, including such information as his monthly income, household income (if applicable), any money or property that he has, and his monthly expenses (all as set forth in the Judicial Council Form FW-1 Request to Waive Court Fees form, a copy of which is attached as exhibit A to the Supplemental Declaration of Gary J. Nevolo filed December 26, 2013) by way of e-mail to the court by the end of business day on January 9, 2014 with a cover letter of the new hearing date. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
